key_insight: A multi-task spatiotemporal deep learning model can infer laser welding penetration state, depth, and weld seam morphology directly from weld-pool images plus process parameters.
engineering_relevance: This enables in-situ quality monitoring and faster defect detection for laser penetration welding, supporting more reliable automated process control.
method: Uses a CNN + state-space model to extract spatial-temporal features from top-view weld pool images.
Combines image features with welding parameters in a multi-task framework.
Includes a dataset construction strategy aimed at improving robustness and generalization.
evidence_signal: On the test set, the model achieved 99.35% penetration-state accuracy, 1.79 mm penetration-depth error, and 95.65% cross-section reconstruction accuracy.
watch_item: Performance may depend on camera quality, welding setup consistency, and the representativeness of the training dataset when deployed on new materials or process conditions.
paper_title: A multi-task spatiotemporal deep neural network for predicting penetration depth and morphology in laser welding
